title: Laneway issues — silver DuckDB table contract
description: Authoritative shape of the silver `issues` table the ingestor must produce. CI compares table columns and DuckDB types against this contract exactly. The `labels` field is flattened to a comma-separated VARCHAR for the silver layer — keep the raw array in bronze if you want to preserve it.

columns.name: issue_id
columns.description: Stable Laneway-issued id. Unique across the dataset.

columns.name: title
columns.description: Issue title — free-text, may contain commas.

columns.name: closed_at
columns.description: Resolution timestamp, NULL while the issue is open. Approx. 30% of rows are NULL.

columns.name: labels
columns.description: Comma-separated, sorted label names (e.g. 'bug,p1'). NULL when no labels. Do NOT take labels[0] — concatenate all of them.

columns.name: comments_count
columns.description: Number of comments on the issue.

columns.name: ingested_date
columns.description: Date of the ingest run (partition key). Required so the silver table records when each row landed.
